questions.scenario: The legacy orders worker needs a vendor package installed at the OS level and writes to a mounted disk. The team cannot package it cleanly into App Service or Container Apps yet. Which compute option is honest?

options.text: A VM, with an explicit operations plan for patching, boot, disk, access, logs, and monitoring.
Azure Functions, because all background work should be event-driven.
App Service, because it removes all OS concerns even when the app depends on them.
Blob Storage, because the worker writes files.

questions.explanation: The VM gives the required control, but it also gives the team more operating responsibility.

questions.scenario: The legacy worker is staying on a VM for now because it needs OS-level control. The team wants the decision record to show what extra work they accepted by choosing a machine. Which responsibilities increase when the team chooses a VM?

options.text: Operating system patching and package management.
Boot diagnostics, VM agent health, disk capacity, and process supervision.
All application bugs disappear because the machine is dedicated.
Network access and identity still need review.

questions.explanation: A VM does not remove application or security work. It adds machine-level work the team must manage.
